summary:
Goldman Sachs (14): SPX target raised to 8k year-end on $340 CY2026 EPS and $385 FY2027; NDX P/E at 24x, SPX P/E at 20x vs. equal-weight 4 turns cheaper; 10yr yield forecast flat at ~4.5%; market breadth snapped back; low stock correlations favor stock-pickers; top-10 weight 39% vs. rising 35% of earnings; sentiment indicator subdued; 2027 capex estimates seen 45pp too conservative.

HSBC (6): Chip stocks tracking 1999 analog; boom-boom macro scenario targets SPX 8k with EM/high-beta leading; global central banks in hawkish pivot mode — policy mistake risk flagged; G7 summit and first Warsh Fed meeting as near-term catalysts.

BofA (1): Hartnett cites Knicks NBA championship win as market-top zeitgeist signal.

Deutsche Bank (1): Monthly market views for June 2026.

Counts: Goldman 14, HSBC 6, BofA 1, Deutsche 1 — 22 items across 4 banks from 60 collected.

banks.theme: GS June 2026 Outlook — SPX 8k target, valuation update, yield flat, breadth recovery, stock-picking regime
